themeDescription: Test your knowledge of gadgets, programming languages, computer history, and digital culture.

questions.question: When was the first iPhone released?

questions.explanation: Mini-LED here refers to multi-zone backlighting on an IPS panel.

questions.explanation: HBM stacks memory dies vertically within one package.

questions.explanation: Thunderbolt emerged from collaboration between Intel and Apple.

questions.explanation: Qi is the widely used inductive wireless-charging standard.

questions.question: Which composer wrote 'Moonlight Sonata'?
questions.explanation: “Moonlight Sonata” was created by Betkhoven.

questions.question: Which Russian tsar is known as 'the Terrible'?
questions.explanation: The historical fact described concerns Ivan IV.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Hamlet'?
questions.explanation: Shekspir is the author or creator of “Hamlet”.

questions.question: Which ancient people created the alphabet?
questions.explanation: Finikiytsy created what is described in the question.

questions.question: Who was the last emperor of Russia?
questions.explanation: Nikolay II played the role stated in the question.

questions.question: Which artist painted the 'Mona Lisa'?
questions.explanation: Leonardo da Vinchi wrote the work named “Mona Lisa”.

questions.question: In what year did World War I begin?
questions.explanation: 1914 Is the date of the historical event named in the question.

questions.question: Who wrote the 'Iliad' and the 'Odyssey'?
questions.explanation: “Iliad” was created by Gomer.

questions.question: Which Russian composer wrote 'The Nutcracker'?

questions.question: Which city was the capital of Ancient Egypt?
questions.explanation: Memfis was the capital of the state named in the question.

questions.question: Who wrote 'War and Peace'?
questions.explanation: “War and Peace” was created by Tolstoy.

questions.question: Which art style did Salvador Dalí represent?
questions.explanation: The features listed in the question are characteristic of Syurrealizm.

questions.question: In what year did the October Revolution take place?
questions.explanation: 1917 Is the date of the historical event named in the question.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Faust'?
questions.explanation: “Faust” was created by Gyote.

questions.question: Which Russian artist painted 'The Morning of the Streltsy Execution'?
questions.explanation: Surikov wrote the work named “The Morning of the Streltsy Execution”.

questions.question: Which ancient city was known for the Colossus of Rhodes?
questions.explanation: Rodos is associated with the landmark or cultural center named in the question.

questions.question: Who was the first person in space?
questions.explanation: Gagarin played the role stated in the question.

questions.question: Which composer wrote 'Symphony No. 5'?
questions.explanation: Betkhoven wrote the work named “Symphony No. 5”.

questions.question: Which Russian writer wrote 'Dead Souls'?
questions.explanation: Gogol is the author or creator of “Dead Souls”.

questions.question: Which city was the capital of the Ottoman Empire?
questions.explanation: Stambul was the capital of the state named in the question.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Anna Karenina'?
questions.explanation: Tolstoy wrote the work named “Anna Karenina”.

questions.question: Which architectural style does Notre-Dame Cathedral represent?
questions.explanation: Notre-Dame Cathedral is Gothic, as shown by its pointed arches and flying buttresses.

questions.question: In what year was Moscow founded?
questions.explanation: The event “In what year was Moscow founded” took place in 1147.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Don Quixote'?
questions.explanation: Servantes wrote the work named “Don Quixote”.

questions.question: Which Russian artist painted 'The Ninth Wave'?
questions.explanation: Ayvazovskiy is the author or creator of “The Ninth Wave”.

questions.question: Which ancient city was known for the Library of Alexandria?
questions.explanation: Aleksandriya is associated with the landmark or cultural center named in the question.

questions.question: Who was the first pope?
questions.explanation: Pyotr played the role stated in the question.

questions.question: Which composer wrote 'The Four Seasons'?
questions.explanation: Vivaldi is the author or creator of “The Four Seasons”.

questions.question: Which Russian writer wrote 'Fathers and Sons'?
questions.explanation: “Fathers and Sons” was created by Turgenev.

questions.question: Which city was the capital of Byzantium?
questions.explanation: Konstantinopol was the capital of the state named in the question.

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Government Inspector'?
questions.explanation: Gogol is the author or creator of “The Government Inspector”.

questions.question: Which painting style represented Klod Mone?
questions.explanation: The features listed in the question are characteristic of Impressionizm.

questions.question: In what year did the Battle of Borodino take place?
questions.explanation: Historical sources date “In what year did the Battle of Borodino take place” to 1812.

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Brothers Karamazov'?
questions.explanation: Dostoevskiy is the author or creator of “The Brothers Karamazov”.

questions.question: Which Russian artist painted 'Barge Haulers on the Volga'?
questions.explanation: “Barge Haulers on the Volga” was created by Repin.

questions.question: Which ancient city was known for the Temple of Artemis?
questions.explanation: Efes is associated with the landmark or cultural center named in the question.

questions.question: Who was the first king of England?
questions.explanation: Alfred Velikiy played the role stated in the question.

questions.question: Which composer wrote 'Requiem'?
questions.explanation: “Requiem” was created by Motsart.

questions.question: Which Russian writer wrote 'The Cherry Orchard'?
questions.explanation: Chekhov wrote the work named “The Cherry Orchard”.

questions.question: Which city was the capital of Ancient China?
questions.explanation: Sian was the capital of the state named in the question.

questions.question: Who wrote 'A Hero of Our Time'?
questions.explanation: “A Hero of Our Time” was created by Lermontov.

questions.question: Which architectural style does the Taj Mahal represent?
questions.explanation: The features listed in the question are characteristic of Mogolskaya arkhitektura.

questions.question: In what year was Magna Carta signed?
questions.explanation: 1215 Is the date of the historical event named in the question.

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Decameron'?
questions.explanation: “The Decameron” was created by Bokkachcho.

questions.question: Which Russian artist painted 'The Rooks Have Returned'?
questions.explanation: Savrasov wrote the work named “The Rooks Have Returned”.

questions.question: Which ancient city was known for the Mausoleum at Halicarnassus?
questions.explanation: Galikarnas is associated with the landmark or cultural center named in the question.

questions.question: Who was the first emperor of China?
questions.explanation: Tsin Shikhuandi played the role stated in the question.

questions.question: Which composer wrote 'Swan Lake'?
questions.explanation: Tchaikovsky composed the score for the ballet Swan Lake, first performed in 1877.

questions.question: Which Russian writer wrote 'The Captain's Daughter'?
questions.explanation: Pushkin is the author or creator of “The Captain”.

questions.question: Which city was the capital of Ancient Rome?
questions.explanation: Rim was the capital of the state named in the question.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Mtsyri'?
questions.explanation: Lermontov wrote the work named “Mtsyri”.

questions.question: Which painting style did Pablo Picasso represent?
questions.explanation: The features listed in the question are characteristic of Kubizm.

questions.question: In what year did the French Revolution take place?
questions.explanation: The event “In what year did the French Revolution take place” took place in 1789.

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Divine Comedy'?
questions.explanation: Dante wrote the work named “The Divine Comedy”.

questions.question: Which Russian artist painted 'Morning in a Pine Forest'?
questions.explanation: Shishkin is the author or creator of “Morning in a Pine Forest”.

questions.question: Which ancient city was known for the Statue of Zeus at Olympia?
questions.explanation: Olimpiya is associated with the landmark or cultural center named in the question.

questions.question: Who was the first president of France?
questions.explanation: Lui Napoleon Bonapart played the role stated in the question.

questions.question: Which composer wrote 'Carmen'?
questions.explanation: Bize is the author or creator of “Carmen”.

questions.question: Which Russian writer wrote 'Oblomov'?
questions.explanation: “Oblomov” was created by Goncharov.

questions.question: Which city was the capital of Ancient Persia?
questions.explanation: Persepol was the capital of the state named in the question.

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Tale of Igor's Campaign'?
questions.explanation: Neizvestnyy avtor is the object, event, or concept named in “Who wrote 'The Tale of Igor's Campaign'”.

questions.question: Which architectural style does St. Peter's Basilica in Rome represent?
questions.explanation: The features listed in the question are characteristic of Barokko.

themeDescription: Test your knowledge of politicians, companies, economics, finance, and major business decisions.

questions.question: When did Putin become acting president of Russia?
questions.explanation: Vladimir Putin became acting president on December 31, 1999, after Boris Yeltsin resigned.

questions.question: BORK is a company from which country?
questions.explanation: BORK is a Russian premium home-appliance brand, despite its German-sounding name.

questions.question: How many countries are members of the UN?
questions.explanation: The United Nations has 193 member states; South Sudan became the most recent member in 2011.

questions.question: What is WeWork's specialization?
questions.explanation: WeWork primarily provides flexible, shared office space and related workplace services.

questions.question: Who owned the main stake in Yukos from 1995 to 2003?
questions.explanation: Mikhail Khodorkovsky controlled Yukos through the Menatep group during that period.

questions.question: Which economic term means a decline in money's purchasing power due to a general rise in prices?
questions.explanation: Inflation is a sustained general rise in prices that reduces what a unit of money can buy.

questions.question: Which country was the first in history to default on its external obligations?
questions.explanation: Spain suspended payments on foreign debt in 1557, often cited as the first sovereign external default.

questions.question: Which tax concept assumes that the rich pay a higher percentage of income than the poor?
questions.explanation: A progressive tax raises the marginal or effective tax rate as taxable income increases.

questions.question: Which bank was recognized as the world's largest investment bank by assets?
questions.explanation: JPMorgan Chase is the expected answer because it has long ranked among the largest global investment banks by assets.

questions.question: Which corporation was the first in history to reach a market capitalization of $1 trillion?
questions.explanation: Apple first crossed the one-trillion-dollar market-capitalization threshold in August 2018.

questions.question: Which G7 country is not a member of the UN Security Council?
questions.explanation: Canada is the only G7 member that is not one of the Security Council’s five permanent members.

questions.question: What is the macroeconomic situation called when unemployment and inflation rise at the same time?
questions.explanation: Stagflation combines stagnant economic growth and high unemployment with persistent inflation.

questions.question: In what year did Saudi Aramco hold the largest IPO in history?
questions.explanation: Saudi Aramco completed its record-setting public offering in 2019.

questions.question: Who authored the concept of the 'invisible hand of the market'?
questions.explanation: Adam Smith used the “invisible hand” metaphor to describe how self-interest can coordinate market activity.

questions.question: Which event led to the global financial crisis of 2008?
questions.explanation: The collapse was triggered by failing US subprime mortgages and securities built from them.

questions.question: Which currency is used in Switzerland?
questions.explanation: The Swiss franc is Switzerland’s official currency and is issued by the Swiss National Bank.

questions.question: Which Russian businessperson founded Wildberries?
questions.explanation: Tatyana Bakalchuk founded Wildberries in 2004 as an online retail business.

questions.question: What is the international organization that monitors corruption levels in countries called?
questions.explanation: Transparency International is known for publishing the Corruption Perceptions Index.

questions.question: What does the abbreviation IPO?
questions.explanation: An IPO is an initial public offering, when a company first sells shares to public investors.

questions.question: Which automotive group owns the Lamborghini brand?
questions.explanation: Lamborghini belongs to the Volkswagen Group through its Audi subsidiary.

questions.question: Who is the founder of the Berkshire Hathaway investment fund?
questions.explanation: Warren Buffett transformed Berkshire Hathaway into the investment conglomerate associated with his leadership.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a cryptocurrency?
questions.explanation: Visa is a global payments network, not a cryptocurrency with its own blockchain-based currency.

questions.question: What is an offshore zone?
questions.explanation: An offshore zone offers businesses or investors preferential tax treatment and often special financial rules.

questions.question: Who wrote the book 'Capital in the Twenty-First Century'?
questions.explanation: Thomas Piketty wrote Capital in the Twenty-First Century, a study of wealth and income inequality.

questions.question: Which businessman took over Tesla after Martin Eberhard left?
questions.explanation: Elon Musk became Tesla’s central business leader after joining in 2004 and later serving as CEO.

questions.question: Which company was founded as an online bookstore?
questions.explanation: Amazon began in 1994 as an online bookstore before expanding into many other categories.

questions.question: In which country was Nestlé founded?
questions.explanation: Nestlé was founded in Switzerland, where Henri Nestlé established the company in Vevey.

questions.question: Which world leader was impeached twice?
questions.explanation: The US House impeached Donald Trump in 2019 and again in 2021, making him the only president impeached twice.

questions.question: Which businessman bought Twitter in 2022?
questions.explanation: Elon Musk completed the acquisition of Twitter, later renamed X, in October 2022.

questions.question: What are sanctions in international politics?
questions.explanation: Sanctions are coercive restrictions—such as trade, financial, or travel limits—used to pressure a state or other target.

questions.question: What is the Russian system for controlling public spending and fighting corruption called?
questions.explanation: Russia’s Federal Treasury administers and controls the execution of public budgets and public funds.

questions.question: Which company ranked first in the world by market capitalization in 2023?
questions.explanation: Apple was the leading company by market value during much of 2023.

questions.question: Which Russian bank was the first to be disconnected from SWIFT in 2022?
questions.explanation: Sberbank was among the first major Russian banks disconnected from SWIFT after the 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

questions.question: Who became the youngest self-made billionaire in history?
questions.explanation: Forbes described Kylie Jenner as the youngest self-made billionaire in 2019, although that characterization was debated.

questions.question: What is the policy of banning certain goods from entering a country called?
questions.explanation: An embargo prohibits or restricts the import or export of specified goods with a country.

questions.question: Which country was the first to introduce universal basic income at the national level?
questions.explanation: Finland ran the widely known national basic-income experiment beginning in 2017.

questions.question: Which Russian oligarch was arrested in London in 2023?
questions.explanation: Mikhail Fridman was arrested in London in 2023 in connection with a UK sanctions-related investigation.

questions.question: Which cryptocurrency ranked first by market capitalization in 2023?
questions.explanation: Bitcoin remained the cryptocurrency with the largest market capitalization in 2023.

questions.question: Which index is called the 'barometer of the American economy'?
questions.explanation: The Dow Jones Industrial Average is commonly treated as a barometer of the US stock market and economy.

questions.question: Which of these billionaires is not part of the 'Big Four' technology companies?
questions.explanation: Elon Musk is not one of the founders or corporate identities normally grouped as Big Tech’s “Big Four.”

questions.question: Which bank was called 'Russia's most resilient bank' before the 2008 crisis?
questions.explanation: KIT Finans was described in Russian financial media as a particularly resilient bank before the 2008 crisis.

questions.question: Which company held the largest IPO in US history in 2014?
questions.explanation: Alibaba raised about $25 billion in 2014, the largest US-listed IPO at that time.

questions.question: Which political term means 'rule by the wealthy'?
questions.explanation: Plutocracy means political power exercised by wealthy people or in their interests.

questions.question: Which of these politicians was not a Nobel Peace Prize laureate?
questions.explanation: Donald Trump has not received the Nobel Peace Prize.

questions.question: What is the economic theory that assumes minimal state intervention called?
questions.explanation: Libertarianism favors individual liberty, private property, and a sharply limited role for the state.

questions.question: Which country was the first to legalize bitcoin as legal tender?
questions.explanation: El Salvador made Bitcoin legal tender in 2021, the first country to do so nationally.

questions.question: Which Russian businessman owns the football club Monaco?
questions.explanation: Dmitry Rybolovlev owns AS Monaco through the company that acquired the club in 2011.

questions.question: Which company developed blockchain technology?
questions.explanation: Satoshi Nakamoto’s Bitcoin paper and software introduced the blockchain design; it was not developed by a conventional company.

questions.question: What is the process of a company's management buying it out called?
questions.explanation: A management buyout occurs when a company’s managers acquire ownership of the business they run.

questions.question: Which of these politicians was not part of the G7?
questions.explanation: Vladimir Putin was not a G7 member; Russia participated in the former G8 before its 2014 suspension.

questions.question: Which Russian bank was the first to be added to the US sanctions list?
questions.explanation: Bank of Moscow was an early Russian bank placed on US sanctions lists.

questions.question: Which country is the largest holder of US government debt?
questions.explanation: Japan is the largest foreign holder of US Treasury securities.

questions.question: What is the economic indicator that reflects all final goods and services called?
questions.explanation: Gross domestic product totals the market value of final goods and services produced in an economy.

questions.question: Which of these billionaires is not on Facebook's board of directors?
questions.explanation: Elon Musk has never served as a member of Meta (formerly Facebook)’s board of directors.

questions.question: Which Russian businessman owns the Brooklyn Nets?
questions.explanation: Mikhail Prokhorov was the Brooklyn Nets’ majority owner from 2010 until he sold the team in 2019.

questions.question: Which company held the largest IPO in China's history?
questions.explanation: Alibaba’s 2014 listing was the largest IPO in Chinese corporate history.

questions.question: What is the economic policy of freezing prices and wages called?
questions.explanation: Income policy uses controls or guidelines on wages and prices to restrain inflation.

questions.question: Which of these politicians was not Russia's finance minister?
questions.explanation: German Gref was minister of economic development, not Russia’s finance minister.

questions.question: Which company was the first to reach a $3 trillion valuation?
questions.explanation: Apple became the first company to reach a three-trillion-dollar market value in January 2022.

questions.question: What is the process of artificially lowering a national currency's exchange rate called?
questions.explanation: Devaluation is an official reduction in a currency’s external value relative to other currencies.

questions.question: Which of these businesspeople is not on the Forbes list?
questions.explanation: Yuri Milner is the expected answer in this quiz’s comparison of the listed Forbes billionaires.

questions.question: Which country was the first to introduce a central bank digital currency?
questions.explanation: China was the first major economy to introduce a central-bank digital-currency pilot at national scale.

questions.question: What is the economic theory based on the ideas of Milton Friedman called?
questions.explanation: Monetarism, associated with Milton Friedman, emphasizes control of the money supply.

questions.question: Which of these politicians was not chair of the Central Bank of Russia?
questions.explanation: Aleksey Ulyukaev was Russia’s economic development minister, not chair of the Central Bank.

questions.question: Which company acquired LinkedIn for $26 billion?
questions.explanation: Microsoft acquired LinkedIn in 2016 for about $26.2 billion.

questions.question: What is the policy of artificially raising a national currency's exchange rate called?
questions.explanation: Revaluation is an official increase in a currency’s exchange rate under a fixed or managed regime.

questions.question: Which of these businesspeople is not on Tesla's board of directors?
questions.explanation: Jeff Bezos has not served on Tesla’s board of directors.

questions.question: Which country was the first to introduce negative interest rates?
questions.explanation: Denmark was an early adopter of negative policy rates, introducing them in 2012.

questions.question: What is the economic theory based on the ideas of John Keynes called?
questions.explanation: Keynesianism follows John Maynard Keynes in supporting demand management and countercyclical public spending.

questions.question: Which of these politicians was not prime minister of Russia?
questions.explanation: Aleksey Kudrin was finance minister and deputy prime minister, but never prime minister of Russia.

questions.question: Which company acquired GitHub for $7.5 billion?
questions.explanation: Microsoft acquired GitHub in 2018 in a transaction valued at $7.5 billion.

questions.question: What is the process of reducing the money supply in an economy called?
questions.explanation: Demonetization reduces or removes money from circulation, sometimes by withdrawing legal-tender notes.

questions.question: Which of these businesspeople is not on Google's board of directors?
questions.explanation: Elon Musk has not served on Alphabet/Google’s board of directors.

questions.question: Which country was the first to introduce a luxury tax?
questions.explanation: France introduced an early luxury tax, imposing additional taxation on luxury goods and consumption.

questions.question: What is the economic theory based on the ideas of Karl Marx called?
questions.explanation: Marxism derives from Karl Marx’s analysis of class conflict, capitalism, and ownership of production.

questions.question: Which of these politicians was not Russia's economy minister?
questions.explanation: Aleksey Kudrin held the finance portfolio rather than the post of economy minister.

questions.question: Which company acquired WhatsApp for $19 billion?
questions.explanation: Facebook acquired WhatsApp in 2014 for roughly $19 billion.

questions.question: What is the process of increasing the money supply in an economy called?
questions.explanation: Money issuance increases the amount of currency or central-bank money in circulation.

questions.question: Which of these businesspeople is not on Amazon's board of directors?
questions.explanation: Warren Buffett has not been a director on Amazon’s board.

questions.question: Which country was the first to introduce universal basic income at the national level?
questions.explanation: Finland conducted the best-known national basic-income experiment beginning in 2017.

questions.question: What is the economic theory based on the ideas of Friedrich Hayek called?
questions.explanation: The Austrian school is associated with Friedrich Hayek and stresses market prices and limited intervention.

questions.question: Which of these politicians was not US Treasury secretary?
questions.explanation: This quiz answer appears to contain an error: Janet Yellen served as US Treasury secretary from 2021 to 2025, so she should not be the “not” choice.

questions.question: Which company acquired Nokia for $7.2 billion?
questions.explanation: Microsoft acquired Nokia’s Devices and Services business in 2014 for about €5.44 billion, often reported as $7.2 billion.

questions.question: What is the process of replacing old banknotes with new ones called?
questions.explanation: Redenomination replaces old banknotes with new units, typically by removing zeros from face values.

questions.question: Which of these businesspeople is not on Berkshire Hathaway's board of directors?
questions.explanation: Jeff Bezos has not served on Berkshire Hathaway’s board of directors.

questions.question: Which country was the first to introduce a carbon tax?
questions.explanation: Finland introduced the world’s first carbon tax in 1990.

questions.question: What is the economic theory based on the ideas of Thorstein Veblen called?
questions.explanation: Institutionalism studies how social, legal, and economic institutions shape economic behavior, reflecting Veblen’s ideas.

questions.question: Which of these politicians was not chair of the US Federal Reserve?
questions.explanation: Steven Mnuchin was US Treasury secretary, not chair of the Federal Reserve.

questions.question: Which company acquired Motorola Mobility for $12.5 billion?
questions.explanation: Google acquired Motorola Mobility in 2012 for $12.5 billion.

questions.question: What is the process of a state refusing to fulfill its debt obligations called?
questions.explanation: A default occurs when a state does not meet its debt-payment obligations.

questions.question: Which of these businesspeople is not on Alibaba's board of directors?
questions.explanation: Elon Musk has not served on Alibaba’s board of directors.

questions.question: Which country was the first to introduce a progressive income tax scale?
questions.explanation: The United Kingdom pioneered a progressive income-tax scale in the early nineteenth century.

questions.question: What is the economic theory based on the ideas of Joseph Schumpeter called?
questions.explanation: Schumpeter’s innovation theory explains economic development through entrepreneurs and creative destruction.

questions.question: Which of these politicians was not president of the World Bank?
questions.explanation: Christine Lagarde led the IMF, whereas she was not president of the World Bank.

questions.question: Which company acquired Skype for $8.5 billion?
questions.explanation: Microsoft acquired Skype in 2011 for $8.5 billion.

questions.question: What is the process of a general decline in prices in an economy called?
questions.explanation: Deflation is a broad, sustained fall in the general price level.

questions.question: Which of these businesspeople is not on Twitter's board of directors?
questions.explanation: Elon Musk became Twitter’s owner but was not a director on its pre-acquisition board.

questions.question: Which country was the first to introduce value-added tax (VAT)?
questions.explanation: France introduced the first value-added tax in 1954.

questions.question: What is the economic theory based on the ideas of Ronald Coase called?
questions.explanation: Transaction-cost theory, associated with Ronald Coase, explains why firms exist and how transactions are organized.

questions.question: Which of these politicians was not head of the IMF?
questions.explanation: Robert Zoellick was president of the World Bank, not managing director of the IMF.

questions.question: Which company acquired YouTube for $1.65 billion?
questions.explanation: Google bought YouTube in 2006 for $1.65 billion in stock.

questions.question: What is the process of simultaneous growth in inflation and unemployment called?
questions.explanation: Stagflation combines rising prices with high unemployment and weak growth.

questions.question: Which of these businesspeople is not on Netflix's board of directors?
questions.explanation: Warren Buffett has not served on Netflix’s board of directors.

questions.question: Which country was the first to introduce inheritance tax?
questions.explanation: The United Kingdom introduced an early modern inheritance tax, making it the expected historical answer.

questions.question: What is the economic theory based on the ideas of James Buchanan called?
questions.explanation: Public-choice theory applies economic analysis to political decision-making and is linked to James Buchanan.

questions.question: Which of these politicians was not head of the ECB?
questions.explanation: Angela Merkel was Germany’s chancellor, not president of the European Central Bank.

questions.question: Which company acquired Instagram for $1 billion?
questions.explanation: Facebook acquired Instagram in 2012 for approximately $1 billion.
